mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and consumer price index indicates robust demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 outside recovery regimes, or returns are flat or negative during confirmed recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumer expenditure, mechanically supporting corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions correlate with negative forward returns in greater than 55% of observations, or rolling IC remains below 0.02 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 900 thousand units, it indicates a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances when HOUST drops below 900.0, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over a naive baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, prompting expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no predictive power, or positive returns occur in greater than 50% of cases where CPIAUCSL growth outpaces FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion regimes, accelerating real GDP growth signals improving corporate profit margins and economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses in late-cycle regimes, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows within the specified early-cycle regime.
